item.title: Clinical combination therapy for IBD and promising co-delivery strategies.
item.description: Inflammopharmacology - Review - Biological agents are the cornerstone of first-line treatment for inflammatory bowel disease. However, approximately 33% of patients either fail to respond initially or experience diminished effica...

item.title: Selenized Probiotic L. brevis 23017 Alleviates Colitis by Modulating the Nrf2-GPX4 Axis to Inhibit Ferroptosis.
item.description: Biological trace element research - Ulcerative colitis (UC) constitutes a principal subtype of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and exhibits escalating global incidence. This study elucidates the therapeutic efficacy and mechanistic ...

item.title: Ovotransferrin Alleviates Ulcerative Colitis by Modulating Ligilactobacillus murinus-Mediated Bile Acid Metabolism via the Ptgs2/JAK-STAT Signaling Axis.
item.description: Journal of agricultural and food chemistry - Ovotransferrin (OVT) is a functionally active protein demonstrated to alleviate ulcerative colitis (UC), although its mechanism remains incompletely understood. Notably, UC is closely associated wi...

item.title: Genome-wide sweeps create ecological units in the human gut microbiome.
item.description: Nature - The human gut microbiome is shaped by diverse selective forces that originate from host and environmental factors and it substantially influences health and disease. Whereas the association of micr...

item.title: Cytotoxic CD4+ T cells and their implication in oral diseases and systemic diseases with oral manifestations.
item.description: International immunopharmacology - Review - T cells are derived from multipotent stem cells in the bone marrow and migrate to the thymus, where they differentiate into two main subpopulations: CD4+ T cells and CD8+ T cells. It is usually bel...

item.title: The Oral-Gut Axis: Bidirectional Interactions Between Microbiome and Diseases.
item.description: Journal of periodontal research - Review - Increased colonization of typically oral microorganisms is frequently observed in the gut mucosa or lumen of individuals with gastrointestinal disorders, including patients with inflammatory bowel ...

item.title: [TNF-alpha Inhibitor-Induced Pulmonary Sarcoidosis].
item.description: Pneumologie (Stuttgart, Germany) - TNF-alpha inhibtors represent an established option for autoimmune disorders such as rheumatoid arthritis, psoriasis and chronic-inflammatory bowel diseases as well as for granulomatous diseases in...

item.title: Medicinal Plants and the Gastrointestinal Microbiota in Chronic Diseases Modulation: A Structured Mechanistic and Translational Review.
item.description: Current microbiology - Review - The gut microbiome supports digestion, immunity, and metabolism; its imbalance (dysbiosis) drives inflammation and metabolic dysfunction, contributing to chronic diseases such as diabetes, cardiova...

item.title: Intestinal epithelial Syndecan-1 maintains mucosal homeostasis in inflammatory bowel disease by enhancing Faecalibacterium prausnitzii biofilm formation.
item.description: Gut microbes - Despite the rising global incidence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), curative therapies remain unavailable. While our previous work implicated the intestinal proteoglycan Syndecan-1 (SDC1) in I...

item.title: Bioengineering a probiotic to bloom during colonic inflammation promotes reliable efficacy in translational models of colitis.
item.description: Gastroenterology - Emerging insights into the gut microbiome have sparked interest in exploring microbial therapeutics for treating inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD). However, no microbial therapeutics have yet shown...

item.title: Early gut-vascular barrier breakdown precedes colitis onset in murine models.
item.description: Cellular and molecular gastroenterology and hepatology - The gut-vascular barrier (GVB) plays a pivotal role in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) pathogenesis. We introduce a novel three-dimensional (3D) multiphoton endomicroscopy (MPEM) approach for real...

item.title: The Overlap Between Crohn's Disease and Intestinal Tuberculosis: A Never-Ending Story.
item.description: Medicina (Kaunas, Lithuania) - Review - The prevalence of Crohn's disease has increased over the last few decades, even in developing countries, whereas that of intestinal tuberculosis has decreased, which places both diseases at an epid...

item.title: The Role of Microbiota and Fecal Transplantation in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Pathogens (Basel, Switzerland) - Systematic review - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs), including ulcerative colitis (UC) and Crohn's disease (CD), are consistently associated with alterations in gut microbial communities, although the extent and ch...

item.title: Biochemistry of Human Gut Microbiota: Related Diseases and Dietary Interactions.
item.description: Molecules (Basel, Switzerland) - Review - The human gut microbiota represents a complex and dynamic ecosystem of trillions of microorganisms that play a fundamental role in maintaining physiological homeostasis, regulating metabolism, and ...

item.title: Bacillus licheniformis Alleviates DSS-Induced Ulcerative Colitis in Mice by Repairing the Intestinal Barrier and Regulating Gut Microbiota and Its Metabolism.

item.title: Gut microbiota-M cell co-culture in inflammatory bowel disease and its therapeutic potential in organoid platforms.
item.description: Frontiers in pharmacology - Review - Dysbiosis of the gut microbiota is a key driver in the onset and persistence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). However, the mechanisms by which microbes influence mucosal immunity via specific e...

item.title: The Structure-Decoding-Conversion-Effect Paradigm of Natural Polysaccharides for Gut Microbiota Remodeling in Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Nutrients - Review - Ulcerative colitis (UC), a chronic inflammatory bowel disease, is closely associated with disturbances in the gut microbiota. Natural polysaccharides, owing to their unique "indigestibility" and pr...
